Title: Nepal's former finance minister arrested for money laundering
Article:
KATHMANDU: Nepal's former finance minister Bishnu Prasad Paudel was arrested late on Monday on charges of money laundering, police said, as the country's Gen Z-backed government steps up a crackdown on alleged corruption under previous administrations.
Paudel, 66, a senior leader of the Communist Party of Nepal (UML) and a key figure in the former K.P. Sharma Oli government, was detained from a hotel in western Nepal, police said.
Police spokesperson Abi Narayan Kafle said Paudel was being brought to Kathmandu and would be handed over to the Department of Money Laundering Investigation.
His arrest comes months after Oli's government was toppled following deadly anti-graft protests in which at least 76 people were killed and more than 2,500 injured, with several government buildings, including parliament, set ablaze.
Oli and his home minister were arrested for failing to prevent the violence. Both are now out on bail.
Prime Minister Balendra Shah, a 36-year-old rapper-turned-politician who took office in March after a landslide mandate promising to tackle corruption, has pledged action against past abuses.
Paudel could not be reached for comment. A UML official said senior party leaders would meet on Tuesday to discuss the development.
Paudel has held multiple ministerial posts across successive governments.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran open to oil supply talks with   Malaysia as waiver takes effect – Envoy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 60-day waiver on the sale of Iranian crude oil in the global market has opened the door for Iran and Malaysia to further strengthen economic cooperation, particularly in the energy sector.
Iranian Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said the waiver allows countries to purchase Iranian crude oil and petroleum products without restrictions, creating opportunities for deeper collaboration.
"Any country now can buy oil and oil production from Iran, and there is no ban on such cooperation. For this reason, Malaysia also can, of course, come and buy oil from Iran," he told reporters on the sidelines of the Global Maritime Economics Conference 2026, organised by the Maritime Institute of Malaysia here today.
The conference, themed "Advancing Resilience and Accelerating Sustainable Maritime Growth", explores trends shaping global maritime economic growth amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.
Valiollah said the latest development provides an opportunity for Malaysia and Iran to expand cooperation across various sectors, particularly the economy, given the longstanding cordial relations between the two countries.
"There is a good chance for both sides to increase cooperation in the economic field, particularly because both countries enjoy very good relations in all areas, including politics, culture, science and technology.
"There are no obstacles, and I am sure technical negotiations can take place between the Iranian and Malaysian sides if Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) or any other company is interested in buying Iranian oil," he added.
Petronas previously had interests in Iran's upstream oil and gas sector. However, based on publicly available information, the national oil company no longer has any active upstream operations in the country following its exit from earlier gas field developments.
On whether the waiver would eventually be made permanent, Valiollah said the current arrangement remains valid for 60 days, with further negotiations expected to determine a final agreement.
"Both sides have agreed to continue negotiations towards a final agreement.
"The United States must show its commitment by implementing its promises and responsibilities," he said.
Meanwhile, state-run news agency Anadolu, headquartered in Ankara, Turkey, reported that Iran has exported 36 million barrels of crude oil since June 15, while a roughly similar volume remains stored onboard vessels offshore awaiting delivery.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari: Sabah, Sarawak need upgraded ports to tap export potential
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sabah and Sarawak's export potential can be better maximised if infrastructure, particularly port capacity, is upgraded to meet international standards, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.
Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said both states possess significant natural resources, but logistical limitations currently hinder their ability to attract larger shipping volumes and international trade routes.
"Infrastructure is key. We have many natural resources in Sabah and Sarawak.
"If we do not have ports of international standing, many vessels will not come, and when they do, they will only bring volume if the ports can handle it," he said in response to a supplementary question from Datuk Seri Shafie Apdal (Warisan–Semporna).
Johari said upgrading ports would require significant investment to expand capacity and improve facilities.
"For example, in Sabah, if a certain volume of containers arrives, the port cannot handle the quantity. If we do not prioritise port development, it will not be viable," he said.
He said the federal government had given Sarawak autonomy to manage its own port development, with continued support from Putrajaya, while similar discussions could be held with Sabah authorities on expansion plans.
"If the state government in Sabah wants to expand, then it should discuss this with the federal government.
"We can look at how Sabah and Sarawak can benefit the country," he said.
He also stressed the importance of moving beyond raw exports and developing downstream processing industries to increase value-added returns.
"As I want to share, palm oil is a classic example of how we successfully transformed a commodity into a high-value export product."
Johari said Malaysia currently produces about 20 million metric tonnes of palm oil, generating up to RM150 billion in export value through downstream products.
"Twenty to 30 years ago, we only exported crude palm oil. Now it has evolved into high-value products."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Brother held after durian orchard dispute leaves sibling seriously injured
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 65-year-old man suffered serious injuries after he was attacked with a parang by his younger brother at a durian orchard in Paya Terubong here on Sunday.
Northeast police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received information on the incident about 3.50pm.
"Initial investigations found that the victim had gone to the orchard in Paya Terubong about 3.30pm.
"Upon arrival, he discovered that various items at a hut in the orchard had been damaged and suspected that his brother was responsible for the destruction.
"The victim confronted the brother over the damaged property, which led to a verbal argument. During the altercation, the suspect allegedly took a parang and slashed the victim three times before fleeing the scene," he said in a statement today.
Rozak said the victim sustained serious injuries and was receiving treatment at Penang Hospital.
He said police later arrested the suspect, the victim's 62-year-old brother, about 6.30pm on the same day.
"With the assistance of the Penang police Criminal Investigation Department K-9 unit, we found the suspect behind a house near the scene.
"He has been remanded for seven days from June 22 to June 28 to assist investigations," he added.
The case is being investigated under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons and Section 18A of the Domestic Violence Act 1994.
Under Section 326 of the Penal Code, those convicted are liable to a jail term of up to 20 years, with a fine or whipping.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BNM'S International reserves at US$130.5 bil as at June 15, 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia's (BNM) international reserves stood at US$130.5 billion as at June 15, 2026.
In a statement today, the central bank said the reserves position is sufficient to finance 4.6 months of imports of goods and services, and is 0.9 times the country's total short-term external debt.
BNM said the gross international reserves comprised foreign currency reserves (US$114.4 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.5 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.4 billion).
Meanwhile, the central bank said its total assets amounted to RM606.30 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including special drawing rights (RM526.90 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM1.84 billion), loans and advances (RM24.68 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM35.23 billion).
BNM said capital and liabilities totalled RM606.30 billion, comprising capital (RM100 million), reserves (RM199.13 billion), currency in circulation (RM183.47 billion), deposits by financial institutions (RM100.32 billion), federal government deposits (RM12.83 billion), other deposits (RM70.31 billion), Bank Negara papers (RM8.89 billion), allocation of special drawing rights (RM26.38 billion), and other liabilities (RM4.89 billion).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt proposes raising max traffic compound from RM300 to RM500
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is proposing to increase the maximum compound rate for traffic offences from RM300 to RM500, effective Jan 1, 2029.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the move is in line with amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 and is intended to improve compliance rather than burden the public or increase government revenue.
"The increase from RM300 to RM500 does not mean all offenders will automatically be issued a RM500 compound.
For compoundable offences, this amendment raises the maximum limit for compound offers.
"The actual rate will still depend on the type and severity of the offence, the settlement period, and the procedures set," he said when tabling the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026 for its second reading in the Dewan Rakyat today.
He said the revised penalties were meant to ensure offences remain relevant and have a stronger deterrent effect.
Taking into account the future enforcement date, he said the transition period would also serve to raise public awareness and encourage better road discipline.
He said among the offences subject to the new compound rate are improper display of registration numbers, using a vehicle without a valid motor vehicle licence, and driving without a valid driving licence.
Other offences include speeding, failure to undergo periodic inspection for certain vehicles, failure to comply with traffic instructions or signals, and other basic road compliance offences.
Further, he said a new Section 42A would be introduced to make street racing or speed testing a specific offence under the Act, allowing enforcement action to be taken without waiting for accidents, injuries or fatalities to occur.
He said the new section provides for a fine of not less than RM2,000 and not more than RM10,000, or imprisonment for up to two years, or both, for a first offence of street racing or participating in speed tests on the road.
For a second or subsequent conviction, harsher penalties will be imposed, namely a fine of not less than RM5,000 and not more than RM20,000, or imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
Loke said amendments to Sections 16 and 17 of the Act do not involve an increase in motor vehicle licence fees or the introduction of new fees, but are instead aimed at ensuring fees are based on the actual area of vehicle usage.
"For example, if a vehicle is regularly used in Peninsular Malaysia, the licence fee will be based on the rate set for the peninsula. Similarly, for vehicles used in Sabah, Sarawak, the Federal Territory of Labuan or Langkawi.
"The fee will be based on the rate applicable in those regions. This means existing licence rates in Sabah and Sarawak, Labuan and Langkawi remain unchanged for vehicles used there."
Loke said the approach ensures a more orderly and fair licensing system that reflects actual vehicle usage, while also addressing revenue leakage when vehicle licence renewals are made in areas with lower rates even though the vehicle is used elsewhere.
Meanwhile, he said amendments to Section 10 would allow all electric motor vehicles in the country to be registered.
"This amendment clarifies that such vehicles must have an electric motor vehicle serial number, with a clear and untampered chassis number.
"This takes into account the development of electric vehicles, which do not have engine numbers like conventional vehicles," he said.
Loke said the amendments to the Act are aimed at strengthening the digitalisation of core services under the Road Transport Department, including the use of electronic documents and notices, as well as enhancing enforcement of serious and frequent offences.
He said it also includes new provisions to address issues of illegal racing and to regulate electric vehicles, special vehicles, modified vehicles, foreign motor vehicles and cross-border vehicles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LBS' Lim Hock San honoured with Asean Lifetime Achievement Award
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: LBS Bina Group Bhd group executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San has been honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award 2026 – Visionary Business Leadership & Community Excellence at the recent Asean Outstanding Business Award 2026, held at the Sunway Resort Hotel in Kuala Lumpur.
The accolade recognises distinguished business leaders whose lifelong contributions go beyond corporate success to generate meaningful social impact, strengthen communities and foster regional cooperation across Asean.
The award celebrates exemplary leaders whose influence has strengthened economic ties, advanced social progress and contributed to shared prosperity across the region.
Widely regarded as one of Malaysia's most respected property entrepreneurs, Lim has transformed LBS from a local construction company into a leading township developer with a diversified portfolio spanning residential, commercial, industrial, hospitality and retail developments.
Lim said this recognition is not a personal achievement alone but a testament to the collective efforts of his colleagues, business partners, stakeholders and the communities who have supported LBS Bina throughout the years.
"At LBS, we have always believed that development is more than constructing buildings; it is about creating opportunities, uplifting lives and fostering communities where people can thrive. I am grateful for the opportunity to contribute to Malaysia's growth and to the broader Asean community," he said.
Over the decades, Lim's visionary leadership and steadfast commitment to nation-building have played a significant role in delivering quality homes, creating sustainable townships and improving the lives of thousands of Malaysians.
Under his stewardship, LBS Bina has delivered more than 58,000 properties and built thriving communities that have positively impacted over 200,000 Malaysians.
Lim's dedication to responsible business practices, community development and long-term value creation reflects the very ideals championed by the award.
Guided by its purpose of "Building Tomorrow Together", the group continues to drive innovation, sustainability and customer-centric development while expanding into strategic growth sectors that support long-term value creation.
The Lifetime Achievement Award is a fitting recognition of Lim's enduring contributions to business, community development and regional progress.
It also reinforces LBS Bina's commitment to carrying forward his vision of building sustainable communities, creating long-term value and contributing meaningfully to Malaysia's development and the broader Asean region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oct 6 hearing set for Altantuya family's judicial review bid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court will hear, on Oct 6, an application for judicial review filed by the family of Mongolian national Altantuya Shaariibuu, seeking to compel police to investigate an additional affidavit and a statutory declaration linked to her murder case.
Lawyer Sangeet Kaur, representing Altantuya's father, Dr Shaariibuu Setev, confirmed the hearing date when contacted.
The matter will be heard before judge Norliza Othman.
On Oct 16 last year, judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman - now a Court of Appeal judge - allowed Shaariibuu to initiate judicial review proceedings after the court was informed that the Attorney-General (A-G) had no objection to the application.
In the application, Shaariibuu named the Inspector-General of Police, the Director of the Criminal Investigation Department, the A-G, and the government as respondents.
He is seeking, among others, a declaration that the respondents' failure to inform him of the current status of investigations conducted in 2019 by the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department into an additional affidavit and a statutory declaration made by former Special Action Unit (UTK) chief inspector Azilah Hadri is unlawful.
The additional affidavit, dated Sept 24, 2024, and the statutory declaration, dated Oct 17, 2019, were relied upon by Azilah in support of his application to review his death sentence at the Federal Court on Oct 10 the same year.
In the documents, Azilah allegedly claimed he received orders from "higher authorities" to carry out a "secret operation" to "kill and eliminate the deceased".
Shaariibuu is seeking a mandamus order to
compel authorities
to complete investigations, if still ongoing, within one month from the date of the court order.
He further seeks an order requiring all respondents to provide detailed updates on the status of the investigations within seven days of the court's decision.
He is also seeking reasons should the investigation papers be classified as "no further action".
On Oct 10, 2024, the Federal Court commuted Azilah's death sentence to
40 years' jail and 12 strokes
of the cane for the murder of Altantuya after allowing his review application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai government's US$12 billion borrowing plan in legal crosshairs as growth falters
Article:
BANGKOK: A Thai government plan to borrow 400 billion baht (US$12.2 billion) to cushion the economy from higher oil prices is facing a legal test that could define the scope of its emergency powers to help struggling households and spur economic growth.
Thailand's Constitutional Court is reviewing whether the borrowing decree issued by Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's administration qualifies as an emergency measure, after accepting a petition from opposition lawmakers last month.
A ruling, expected by July 16, could shape both the fate of the loan and the pace of government support as growth weakens and fiscal room narrows in Southeast Asia's second-largest economy.
A court ruling that disrupts the borrowing plan "could add another layer of uncertainty to the growth outlook," said Lavanya Venkateswaran, senior Asean economist at OCBC, though solid exports and some external tailwinds could support near-term momentum.
Only months into its four-year term, Anutin's government is already facing discontent as a price shock driven by the war in Iran deepens Thailand's farm debt crisis, Reuters has reported.
The cabinet approved the decree in May, splitting funds equally between relief measures such as a 176 billion baht consumer subsidy scheme and funding a longer-term shift towards clean energy.
Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut, leader of the main opposition People's Party, which was among those that petitioned the court, told reporters last month he supported the transition to clean energy but opposed using an emergency decree to push it through.
"The government has done nothing wrong," Paradorn Prissananantakul, a minister attached to the prime minister's office, told Reuters. "I am confident the decree will be passed."
Even if the court strikes down the decree, measures already rolled out under the plan will stand, Paradorn said.
RISK OF POLITICAL FALLOUT
Finance Minister Ekniti Nitithanprapas said last week the borrowing decree is essential for the energy transition, citing Thailand's heavy reliance on imported energy – nearly 10 per cent of gross domestic product - as a key vulnerability.
"If we do not act now, Thailand risks losing its competitiveness over time," he said.
The central bank expects the fiscal push from the borrowing to lift economic growth this year by half a percentage point to two per cent. The economy expanded 2.4 per cent last year, lagging regional peers.
The court could also allow the cost-of-living relief portion of the borrowing plan to stand while striking down the energy component, with limited impact on government operations, said political scientist Stithorn Thananithichot of Chulalongkorn University.
"The government still has some room on the spending side to keep this project going. If there is no money, it can be halted… possibly shelving the project and waiting to use the fiscal 2027 budget," he said, referring to the budget that will start in October.
Even a ruling against the entire decree would have largely political, not destabilising, consequences due to the government's parliamentary majority, Stithorn said.
"Administratively, the government can explain the issue, but politically it will face heavy criticism," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aeon Credit to ride on premium bike trend
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd is banking on growing demand for premium motorcycles and stronger brand visibility to drive further expansion of its motorcycle financing business in the financial year ending Feb 28, 2027 (FY27).
Managing director and chief executive officer Daisuke Maeda said motorcycle financing remains one of its core growth segments, supported by stable asset quality and increasing demand across higher-value categories.
He said non-performing loans in the motorcycle financing portfolio have remained stable, allowing the company to continue expanding its presence in the market.
"We are continuing our growth strategy because motorcycle financing asset quality remains healthy," he said at a press conference after the company's 29th annual general meeting today.
Maeda said a key focus area is the superbike segment, particularly motorcycles with engine capacities exceeding 250cc.
He noted that demand for larger-capacity motorcycles has been rising as Malaysian consumers' income levels improve, creating opportunities for Aeon Credit to capture a larger share of the market.
"The demand in this segment is increasing and sales have almost doubled," he added.
Apart from product expansion, the company is strengthening its brand presence through motorsports sponsorships, including its support for young motorcycle racing talent Hakeem Danish.
Chairman Ng Eng Kiat said the sponsorship aligns with Aeon Credit's strong presence in the motorcycle financing sector and reflects its commitment to supporting local talent and the broader motorsports ecosystem.
He said the company is open to continuing its sponsorship of Hakeem, subject to periodic reviews and commercial considerations as the rider progresses in his career.
In addition, Aeon Credit is pursuing cross-selling opportunities through motorcycle-related credit card offerings to deepen customer engagement and broaden its revenue streams.
"It is some avenue where we always move towards championing local causes and local talent," Ng said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar tells Johor exco to question BN, PN over state revenue claims
Article:
TANGKAK: Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim urged a Johor executive councillor to question the previous Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional federal administrations regarding claims that the state did not receive its fair share of revenue.
Without naming the exco member, the prime minister said the allegations actually referred to events that occurred under the governance of the two coalitions before and after 2018.
"When I talked about oil from Turkmenistan, Bintulu Port, and programmes for the rakyat, why did the exco member launch these criticisms? He has only just become an exco member.
"My job is not to criticise people. I did not mention your name or your position, so why are you snapping?
"He claims the prime minister does not know how to manage the country and takes money from Johor without giving it back. Where did you study economics?
"He criticised me. Yes, I am the prime minister, but the deputy prime minister is the Umno president. I work well with him, so do not say we are sidelining Johor.
"Come down here to Bukit Gambir and ask the Malays, Chinese, and Indians. Sometimes they are smarter than those who give long speeches," Anwar said at the Pakatan Harapan candidate announcement ceremony for the Johor election in Bukit Gambir here last night.
He also advised the parties involved to check the facts before making any criticisms.
He said his administration always welcomed feedback from various parties, acknowledging that there were still shortcomings that needed to be addressed.
"Look at the facts. Do you think we are perfect and the Madani government cannot be criticised? In cabinet meetings, ask any minister, many criticise me. I have to listen to everything. I do not just consider the voices of Umno, DAP, PBS, or GRS," he said.
Recently, caretaker Johor Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman Mohd Hairi Mad Shah urged the federal government led by the prime minister to
stop dilly-dallying
and making accusations against the state.
Hairi made the criticism following a statement by Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, who had said the federal government should return a
more reasonable portion
of the revenue collected from the state.
His royal highness said this would ensure that the development of Johor and the welfare of its people could be carried out more effectively.
Hairi claimed that revenue was sucked out of Johor but was not returned fairly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Most SMEs still in experimental stages of AI maturity, says report
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nearly 70 per cent of small and medium enterprises (SMEs)  remain in the experimental or opportunistic stages of artificial intelligence (AI) maturity despite growing investment and widespread use of AI tools, according to a new global report commissioned by SAS and IDC.
The report, titled AI for Small and Midsized Businesses: Closing the Readiness-Reality Gap, is based on a survey of more than 1,600 leaders of SMEs across 28 countries.
It found that while AI adoption is becoming increasingly widespread, many businesses are still unable to translate pilot projects into scalable, organisation-wide impact.
IDC vice-president of research Daniel-Zoe Jimenez said SMEs need to move beyond disconnected pilot projects and align their data, people and resources to realise the full value of AI.
"Experimenting with the technology is one thing. Deploying it strategically and sustainably is quite another," he said in a statement.
The survey also highlighted a range of challenges across industries, including fragmented data, inconsistent execution, regulatory hurdles and limited organisation-wide adoption, which continue to hinder efforts to scale AI.
While banks are relatively advanced in AI strategy and governance, many still struggle to move beyond pilot programmes and achieve consistent organisation-wide deployment.
Insurance firms are actively applying AI to operational challenges but face scaling difficulties due to fragmented data and uneven execution.
In the public sector, government agencies demonstrate strong planning and oversight, but legacy systems and data silos continue to slow implementation.
Healthcare organisations are experimenting with AI to improve efficiency, although data complexity, regulatory requirements and skills shortages keep adoption at an early stage.
Meanwhile, life sciences organisations see significant AI potential, but complex data environments and regulatory demands limit broader adoption beyond specialised teams.
Overall, the report identified what it described as a "readiness-reality gap", where AI ambitions among SMEs are not matched by organisational preparedness.
Key obstacles include fragmented systems, isolated AI initiatives, limited workforce skills, and weak governance and return-on-investment measurement frameworks.
To address these challenges, the report introduces an AI Readiness Index that assesses organisations across the planning, building, enabling and executing stages, categorising maturity into four levels: experimental, opportunistic, structured and integrated.
SAS senior vice-president of global channels John Carey said SMEs do not need more hype around AI but practical outcomes that generate meaningful returns on investment.
"This research shows that AI adoption is already widespread, but operationalising AI at the company level remains a challenge.
"SAS outlines a path forward with tools and resources tailored to the needs of small and midsized business customers," he said.
Alongside the report, SAS has launched an AI Readiness Calculator to help SMEs assess their current maturity level, identify gaps and receive tailored recommendations for improving their AI capabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Developer fails in bid to challenge Genting road privatisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A developer has failed in its bid to challenge the privatisation of the main access road to Genting Highlands after the High Court dismissed its application.
GM Aero Support Sdn Bhd had sought to invalidate land titles issued over the road, arguing that the move affected access to its planned commercial development in Bentong.
Judge Roslan Mat Nor ruled that while the company was an aggrieved party and had filed its appeal within the prescribed period, it had pursued the matter under the wrong legal provision.
The dispute centred on eight parcels of land forming the main access road to Genting Highlands, which were alienated and issued titles in March 2023 by Pahang State Authority to Genting Highlands Bhd for use as a private road.
GM Aero claimed it owned neighbouring land originating from the same parent title known as Lot 8757 and had obtained planning approval from the Bentong Municipal Council to develop the area for commercial purposes.
The company said it only became aware of the title issuance on July 11, 2023, after documents surfaced during separate High Court proceedings.
It filed the suit against the Pahang Land and Mines director, the Pahang Registrar of Titles, the Bentong Land Administrator, and Genting Highlands Bhd.
Roslan, in his grounds of judgment, said the court agreed that GM Aero was a party aggrieved by the privatisation as the road represented the main route to its land.
"It cannot be denied that the plaintiff has an interest in the access road to its land.
"The plaintiff is affected by the decision of the defendants and falls within the definition of an aggrieved party who may bring an appeal under Section 418 of the National Land Code (NLC)," he said.
However, the judge said the appeal ultimately failed as the impugned decision was not made by the Land and Mines director, Registrar of Titles or Land Administrator, which are the authorities covered under Section 418.
Instead, the judge found that the alienation of the land had been approved by the State Authority.
"This court is of the view that it is crystal clear that the decision concerning the lands was made by the State Authority.
"This means that the decision is not covered by the provisions of Section 418 of the NLC and cannot be appealed under that provision," he said.
He added that such decisions could potentially be challenged through other legal procedures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chin Tong, Cai Tung among DAP veterans dropped for Johor polls
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU: Several prominent DAP figures will not defend their seats in the upcoming Johor state election.
According to the candidate list announced by Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Tangkak last night, they include Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong.
Liew, who is DAP strategic director and Iskandar Puteri member of parliament, was the Perling assemblyman. His seat will be contested by Alan Tee.
Liow Cai Tung, who has held the Johor Jaya seat for three consecutive terms since the 2013 general election, was also dropped, making way for Lee Wern Yiing.
In Mengkibol, Chew Chong Sin will step aside for young lawyer Chu Poh Yee after serving two terms.
It is understood that Liow and Chew were not renominated as they are slated to contest parliamentary seats in the next general election.
PH is also fielding a new face in Penggaram, where Felicia Poh will take over from Gan Peck Cheng.
Gan, the former Johor state assembly deputy speaker, previously said she intended to retire from politics.
In Skudai, Kartiyaini Jeyapalan will seek to defend the seat for DAP after its former representative, Marina Ibrahim, announced on May 31 that she would retire from politics.
The Election Commission has set nomination day for June 27 and early voting for July 7, ahead of polling day on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retirees recount losing life savings to alleged investment scams
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Some 100 scam victims who gathered outside the Federal Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) today said they lost their retirement savings accumulated over decades of work.
They wanted police to speed up investigations and bring those responsible to justice as soon as possible.
Among them was retired teacher Che Kob Che Ngah, 68, from Pasir Mas, Kelantan, who claimed he lost RM80,000 after investing in a cooperative that allegedly promised monthly returns.
Che Kob said he invested his pension and retirement gratuity after being introduced to the scheme by friends. He said the cooperative initially paid dividends for about six months before payments stopped altogether.
"The investment came from my retirement savings. I am 68 years old now and I hope this case can be resolved while I am still alive," he spoke to reporters outside the CCID headquarters at Jalan Tun Razak here today.
Che Kob said around 100 victims linked to the cooperative had lodged police reports since 2020 but were still waiting for progress in the case.
Another victim, known only as Aisyah, 69, said losing nearly RM500,000 had left a lasting emotional impact.
The Kuala Lumpur resident said the money came from her Employees Provident Fund (EPF) savings accumulated over 34 years of employment with a private company.
After retiring, she withdrew almost RM900,000 from her EPF account and deposited it in a bank before being introduced to several investment opportunities that promised attractive returns.
"I believed them because they were very convincing. In the initial stages, there were returns, so I felt reassured.
"But in the end, nearly RM500,000 disappeared just like that," she said.
Aisyah said she had never revealed the loss to her family because she did not want to burden them.
"I never told anyone. Even my siblings don't know.
"I'm afraid that if I think about it too much, I'll become even more stressed," she said, struggling to hold back tears.
She said the financial loss had become even harder to bear because she now relies on her remaining savings in Tabung Haji while caring for her 90-year-old mother, who is bedridden after suffering a stroke.
Living with her mother in Cheras, Aisyah said she spends between RM950 and RM1,200 a month on necessities including adult diapers, medication and caregiving expenses.
"I withdraw money little by little from what I have left.
"Right now, all I think about is how to continue living and taking care of my mother," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six arrested after two men slashed at Jelutong temple
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Six men have been arrested following a parang attack at a temple in Jelutong that left two men with severe injuries.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the incident occurred about 4.30am on Sunday at a Chinese temple in Jelutong.
He said preliminary investigations found that the attack stemmed from a misunderstanding between one of the victims and a group of men at a nightclub earlier that morning.
"The 31-year-old victim was accused by one of the suspects of stealing a mobile phone although investigations indicate otherwise.
"The group subsequently followed the victim to the temple, where he was attacked by about seven people armed with parang," he said at the state police headquarters here today.
Lim said a 64-year-old Chinese temple caretaker who attempted to intervene and stop the assault was also attacked.
"The first victim suffered the loss of an index finger, while the second victim lost a little finger. Both also sustained slash wounds during the incident.
"However, both victims are in stable condition," he added.
Lim said police arrested six men, aged between 26 and 38, on the same day in several locations around the Northeast district.
He said they also seized a parang, believed to have been used in the attack, six mobile phones, clothing suspected to have been worn during the incident, two motorcycles, a car, a helmet, keys and a remote control for further investigation.
"Four of the suspects have admitted their involvement in the incident. We have also identified several other suspects who are still at large and efforts are underway to arrest them as soon as possible," he said.
Checks revealed that some of the suspects have criminal and drug-related records, although urine screening tests on all six suspects returned negative results.
The George Town magistrate's court granted a seven-day remand order from June 22 to June 28 to facilitate investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons and Section 120B of the Penal Code for criminal conspiracy.
Lim said police were also investigating whether those involved had links to organised crime groups.
"We are looking into their background, including possible involvement in gangsterism.
"If such elements are found, we will take firm action against them," he said.
Police have urged anyone with information on the incident to contact investigating officer Inspector Mohamad Azwan Mahmud Safbri at 010-7845429 or the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police urged to expedite probes into 18 suspected scam companies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) today gathered more than 100 investment scam victims to urge police to expedite investigations into 18 companies and investment platforms allegedly linked to syndicated fraud schemes.
The victims, accompanied by MHO representatives, submitted 18 memorandums to the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID), calling for investigations to be completed and suspects prosecuted without further delay.
MHO secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the organisation had received complaints involving hundreds of victims nationwide, with some cases dating back several years.
He said while many police investigation papers had already been opened, victims were becoming increasingly frustrated over the lack of prosecutions.
"Victims do not just want investigation papers opened. They want investigations completed and submitted for prosecution.
"Some have been waiting for two, three or even four years without seeing those responsible brought before the courts," he said during the gathering.
Hishamuddin said MHO had been informed that some investigation papers were still being completed before being forwarded to prosecutors.
He stressed that the organisation was not accusing police of failing to investigate, noting that many other cases brought by MHO had resulted in prosecution.
However, he said victims involved in the 18 cases wanted greater urgency due to the substantial losses suffered and the lengthy wait for action.
The complaints involve various schemes, including forex trading, cryptocurrency investments, overseas funds, gold, property, shares, cooperatives, vending machine businesses and debt management programmes.
Many victims alleged they were promised lucrative returns, fixed profits and guaranteed capital repayments before eventually losing access to their funds.
Hishamuddin said the cases differed from online scam cases because many victims personally knew or had met those behind the schemes through investment presentations and promotional events.
"These victims know who the masterminds are. They attended presentations and dealt directly with them.
"They simply want investigations completed and those responsible charged," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Peru leftist presidential candidate demands annulment of overseas vote
Article:
LIMA: Peruvian left-wing presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez called on Monday for the annulment of overseas votes in the runoff election in which he is narrowly trailing his conservative rival.
With almost all the ballots counted, right-wing candidate Keiko Fujimori held 50.11 per cent of the vote, versus Sanchez's 49.88 per cent, according to the National Office of Electoral Processes.
Fujimori is ahead of Sanchez by just over 40,000 votes.
But before declaring a winner, election officials need to review contested tally sheets of around 82,000 votes.
"We have filed a request for annulment so that the National Jury of Elections may declare null and void the elections held by the 119 consular offices," Sanchez said on X.
"The electoral process has been seriously affected by the changes introduced at the request of the executive branch (Foreign Ministry), specifically in the presidential runoff," he said, without providing evidence.
Sanchez alleges administrative irregularities in the handling of the overseas vote, which represents around 300,000 ballots, by the electoral authority.
The overseas vote largely favoured Fujimori, who was propelled by massive support from voters in the United States and Japan.
According to Sanchez, if votes cast abroad were excluded, he would hold a lead of around 25,000 votes over Fujimori.
The June 7 runoff pitted the daughter of the late former president Alberto Fujimori against Sanchez, the political heir of former president Pedro Castillo.
The winner takes office on July 28 for a five-year term.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM25.2mil allocated for solar LED lights at 32 high-risk highway locations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has allocated RM25.2 million for the initial phase of installing solar-powered LED street lights at 32 high-risk and accident-prone locations along highway networks nationwide.
Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the initiative, under the Madani Monitoring programme, involves the installation of 3,000 light poles to enhance road users' safety at the identified locations.
He said the project is a proactive measure by the Works Ministry, together with the Finance Ministry and the Malaysian Highway Authority (MHA), in response to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's call when he announced the related allocation in the 2026 Budget in October last year.
"The government takes road accidents very seriously. The original allocation mentioned earlier was RM30 million, but for this initial phase, we are allocating RM25 million first. The remaining funds will be disbursed after we assess requirements from time to time.
"The letter of acceptance (LoA) has been handed over to the selected contractor today, which means physical works on site can commence immediately. The project is targeted for full completion by the end of November this year," he said.
He said this at a press conference after handing over the LoAs to four contractors appointed to implement the project, here today.
Also present were Works Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Azman Ibrahim, MHA chairman Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, MHA director-general Datuk Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff and Public Works Department director-general Datuk Ibrahim Esa.
Nanta said the total value of the LoAs issued today amounted to RM25,248,813.08.
Of the amount, 1,005 light poles costing RM8.11 million will be installed at 12 locations along the North-South Expressway (E1) (North), between the Sungai Buloh Interchange and the Gurun Interchange.
Another 567 light poles, with a contract value of RM5.09 million, will be installed at six locations along the Seremban-Port Dickson Expressway (E29) and the North-South Expressway (E2) (South), covering the stretch between the Senawang Interchange and the Pedas Linggi Interchange.
Along the E2 (South) stretch between the Ayer Keroh Interchange and the Yong Peng (North) Interchange, 738 light poles will be installed at five locations at a cost of RM6.25 million.
The remaining 690 light poles, valued at RM5.77 million, will be installed at nine locations covering the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (E8) (between Karak and Bentong), the East Coast Expressway Phase 1 (between the Chenor Interchange and the Gambang Interchange), and the East Coast Expressway Phase 2 (between the Bukit Besi Interchange and the Ajil Interchange).
Meanwhile, Hadzmir said MHA uses the MH Roads system to monitor accident data in detail to identify black spots.
"Of the 32 locations identified, nine are officially designated black spots with a serious accident weighting, while the remaining locations are areas with high-risk potential.
"The installation of these solar-powered LED lights will be closely monitored to assess trends in the reduction of accident rates. Upon completion of the project, maintenance responsibilities will be handed over to the respective highway concessionaires under the supervision of the LLM," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E-hailing driver remanded over assault of ex-wife outside police station
Article:
KAJANG: Police have obtained a three-day remand order against an e-hailing driver suspected of assaulting his former wife at a public car park outside the Bandar Baru Bangi police station.
District police chief Assistant Commissionet Naazron Abdul Yusof said the remand order was granted by the Bandar Baru Bangi magistrate's court today.
"The suspect has been remanded for three days to assist investigations into the case," he said when contacted.
Naazron said investigations into the motive and circumstances surrounding the incident were still ongoing.
The suspect, who is in his 20s,
was arrested
at 6.20pm yesterday by dristrict Criminal Investigation Department policemen.
Police received a report on the alleged assault at 2.51pm on June 22 and later detected a video of the incident that had gone viral on social media platform Threads.
The suspect is the victim's former husband.
The case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt and Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation.
Police have urged anyone with information on the incident to contact investigating officer Inspector Afiq Rifdi Mohd Ami at 011-29667474.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to name Johor candidates tomorrow?
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional is expected to unveil its candidates for the upcoming state election tomorrow morning.
According to a poster believed to have been issued by the Johor BN secretariat, the candidates will be announced at the Johor Umno headquarters, with senior national and state coalition leaders expected to attend.
When contacted, the Johor BN secretariat neither confirmed nor denied the poster's authenticity, saying only that updates on the candidate announcement would be provided from time to time.
The Johor election is shaping up to be a three-cornered contest, with BN, Pakatan Harapan and Perikatan Nasional expected to contest all 56 seats.
Previously, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the
coalition's candidate selection criteria
centred on service, acceptability and familiarity rather than age.
"A candidate must be local, known in the constituency and accepted by the community. Most importantly, the candidate must have a strong service track record and be winnable," he said in an interview last week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 12 held in Penang over illegal online betting during World Cup crackdown
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have arrested 12 men and seized nearly RM9,000 in cash in a statewide crackdown on illegal online betting during the Fifa World Cup.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the arrests were made under Op Soga XI, a nationwide operation targeting unlicensed sports betting and online gambling activities.
He said the operation, spearheaded by the Criminal Investigation Department with support from federal police in Bukit Aman and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), is being conducted from June 11 to July 19.
It was recently reported that 58 people, including four women, were
nabbed for illegal sports betting
and online gambling activities linked to the 2026 World Cup.
Federal police CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the arrests were made during 52 raids conducted nationwide under Op Soga XI.
"In Penang, we have carried out 12 raids so far and arrested 12men, aged between 27 and 64, for suspected involvement in illegal online gambling and football betting activities," Lim told newsmen at the state police headquarters here today.
He said the suspects were detained in separate operations across the state.
He said investigations found that most of the suspects placed or managed bets through online platforms and mobile applications.
"We have identified the applications and websites being used for these activities.
"Mobile phones believed to have been used in the gambling transactions were also seized during the operations," he added.
Police also confiscated RM8,692 in cash, believed to be linked to the gambling activities.
Lim said authorities had identified 12 websites, platforms, mobile applications and social media accounts used to facilitate football betting and promote online gambling during the World Cup period.
"Action will be taken with the assistance of MCMC to block the websites and remove the online content involved," he said.
All suspects have been released on police bail pending the completion of investigations.
The cases are being investigated under Sections 6(1) and 6(3) of the Betting Act 1953, as well as Section 4(1)(c) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953.
Lim said investigations would also examine possible offences under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
He warned that police would intensify enforcement efforts throughout the World Cup period and would not tolerate any form of illegal gambling activity.
"Online gambling continues to attract syndicates seeking to profit from major sporting events. We will continue taking firm action against those involved," he said.
Lim also urged the public to channel information on gambling activities to the police to enable swift enforcement action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kerjaya Prospek lands RM488mil Andaman project from E&O
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd has secured a RM488.4 million contract for the construction of a second marina-edge residence at Andaman Island.
The company said its wholly-owned Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd will develop two 46-storey serviced apartment towers.
They will boast a total of 1,080 residential units equipped with basement and podium parking facilities, commercial space and recreational amenities.
Construction is due to start on July 1 this year and is targeted for completion in 42 months.
The contract was awarded by Persada Mentari Sdn Bhd, the indirect subsidiary of Eastern & Oriental Bhd, through an arm's-length transaction conducted on normal commercial terms comparable to those offered to unrelated third parties.
Kerjaya Prospek chief executive officer and executive director Tee Eng Tiong said: "As one of Penang's most prominent waterfront developments, Andaman Island represents a transformative project for the state, and we are proud to contribute to bringing this vision to life,"
The latest deal marks the eighth win for the company this year, bringing its year-to-date contract securement to RM2.1 billion for the financial year 2026.
"Together with our outstanding order book of RM5 billion, the group remains well-positioned with strong earnings visibility and a healthy pipeline of construction projects over the coming years," Tee added.
Meanwhile, Kerjaya Prospek completed the acquisition of a 49 per cent stake in Aspen Vision Land Sdn Bhd to support the Aspen Vision City development in Batu Kawan, Penang.
It also secured full ownership of Tanjung Bungah Development Sdn Bhd through the purchase of the remaining stake.
Kerjaya Prospek said the transactions will strengthen its presence in Penang, expand its landbank and development portfolio, and provide exposure to strategically located projects with long-term growth potential, supporting sustained value creation over time.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Work hard, avoid bickering, Anwar tells PH machinery in Johor [WATCH]
Article:
TANGKAK: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the coalition's leaders and election machinery in Johor to avoid bickering with parties that are part of the federal government.
Anwar, who is also prime minister, said PH must focus on working hard and addressing issues effectively to secure victory in the state election.
"We can win in Johor, but we need to work hard and address issues wisely and properly. We want elected representatives who prioritise the interests of the Johor people.
"I do not want bickering, especially with our partners whose parties are in the federal government. It gives me a headache.
"I am the leader. When the children fight, the father gets a headache. In meetings, everything is fine, but when they leave, they fight," he said after announcing PH's candidates for the Johor election last night.
Anwar said PH was not intimidated by the contest, which will see PH take on Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional in all seats. He said all of the coalition's candidates were Johoreans.
Last night, he unveiled PH's full slate of 56 candidates, about 90 per cent of whom are first-time contenders in the state election.
Two PH members of parliament, Suhaizan Kayat (Pulai) and Onn Abu Bakar (Batu Pahat), will also contest state seats.
Suhaizan of Amanah will contest Larkin, while PKR's Onn has been fielded in Senggarang.
The Election Commission has set July 11 as the polling day for the Johor election. Nomination is scheduled for Saturday, while early voting will take place on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Topmix partners RHB, SP Setia to expand digital renovation platform myRuma
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Topmix Bhd has teamed up with RHB Bank, RHB Islamic Bank and SP Setia to expand its myRuma digital renovation platform by offering financing solutions and access to residential property buyers.
Under the partnerships, RHB Bank and RHB Islamic Bank will provide home and renovation financing through a milestone-based payment framework, allowing funds to be released in stages to verified renovation professionals while improving transparency during renovation works.
Separately, Topmix's collaboration with SP Setia will bring myRuma into the residential township segment through the Setia EcoHill 2 development, giving homeowners access to integrated renovation solutions and a curated network of design-and-build professionals.
The myRuma platform was developed by Topmix's wholly owned subsidiary, Luma International Sdn Bhd.
Topmix managing director and Luma chief executive officer Jack Teo Quek Siang said the group has invested RM1.5 million in capital expenditure (capex) to develop the platform.
"We have received more than 100 enquiries already within two to three weeks of its soft launch in May. The feedback has been very positive from homeowners, renovation professionals and interior designers," he said at a press conference today.
Teo said the platform was created to address trust and reliability issues in Malaysia's fragmented renovation industry. It is built around four pillars: financial assurance, quality governance, risk protection and a verified network of renovation professionals.
He added that Malaysia's home renovation and improvement market is worth more than RM30 billion annually, presenting significant growth potential for the business.
Initially, myRuma will generate revenue through commissions from contractors and marketing or listing fees charged to ecosystem partners.
Additional income streams are expected to come from merchandising and agency-related services over time.
Executive director and chief operating officer Jessie Tan Lee Hong said all contractors onboarded onto myRuma must comply with the Reno Playbook, which serves as a standardised renovation quality governance manual.
She said contractors are required to upload progress photos throughout the renovation process while myRuma's quality control team conducts inspections before each milestone payment is released.
"If the contractor cannot fulfil our quality checking, they have to redo the work until it meets the required standard.
"If they are still unable to comply, we will change the renovator. That's why myRuma is here. We give homeowners assurance and peace of mind," she said.
Coinciding with the launch, myRuma also introduced the Reno Playbook, which it described as Malaysia's first renovation quality manual designed to promote consistency and ensure renovation works comply with professional construction standards.
Tan said homeowners can also monitor renovation progress remotely through a dashboard displaying inspection updates and project photos, allowing them to track work without visiting the site.
Meanwhile, in terms of its competitive advantage, Tan said Topmix's long-standing relationships with contractors and interior designers through its decorative surface business enabled myRuma to establish a trusted network of verified renovation professionals.
Unlike conventional renovation marketplaces, she said myRuma integrates financing, third-party quality inspections, insurance protection and project governance into a single ecosystem.
"Many platforms are listing platforms. We are an ecosystem. We connect homeowners, contractors, financing partners, quality assurance providers and insurance partners under one platform," she said.
Tan said the integrated model also helps resolve common disputes between homeowners and contractors by providing an independent third party to oversee renovation quality and milestone-based payments.
Looking ahead, Teo said myRuma plans to expand beyond the Klang Valley.
The platform has already attracted interest from property developers and real estate agents in other markets, including Johor Bahru.
"The establishment of myRuma reflects Topmix's long-term vision of complementing its core business with innovative home solutions while creating new avenues of sustainable growth," Teo said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR MPs: Give Parliament real vetting power, not just 'right to comment' on public prosecutor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Eight PKR lawmakers have called for a robust parliamentary vetting process in the proposed constitutional amendments to separate the roles of the attorney-general and public prosecutor.
Subang member of parliament Wong Chen said the latest draft still fails to address what they describe as a "fundamental issue": Parliament's role in the appointment of the public prosecutor.
"What we must have is a bipartisan parliamentary vetting process for nominees proposed by the Judicial and Legal Service Commission.
"This should involve open and transparent hearings in which Parliament, on behalf of the rakyat, can rigorously examine nominees on their competence, experience, independence, and, most importantly, integrity," he said in a press conference at the Dewan Rakyat here today.
The other seven MPs are S. Kesavan (Sungai Siput), Rodziah Ismail (Ampang), Lee Chean Chung (Petaling Jaya), Zahir Hassan (Wangsa Maju), Hassan Karim (Pasir Gudang), Datuk Muhammad Bakhtiar Wan Chik (Balik Pulau), and Tan Kar Hing (Gopeng).
Wong said nominees should also be required to submit a full, independently audited asset declaration during the nomination process.
"The nation cannot afford a situation where a powerful and senior public office holder later faces questions and investigations over issues of integrity and personal financial interests," he said.
Wong said the group also rejects Clause 18 of the proposed amendments, which only requires the Judicial and Legal Service Commission to inform Parliament of nominees for public prosecutor and allows Parliament to submit comments.
He said this proposed process of merely informing and allowing comments does not constitute meaningful parliamentary vetting oversight.
He said on Feb 26, the group had proposed that all nominees for public prosecutor undergo confirmation hearings before a bipartisan parliamentary special select committee, which would be broadcast publicly.
They also proposed that nominees who pass the committee stage be voted on in the Dewan Rakyat, requiring a simple majority for confirmation, before being referred back to the Judicial and Legal Service Commission for further action.
Wong said the Madani government currently holds 151 seats in Parliament, while the constitutional amendment requires 148 votes for a two-thirds majority.
"We urge the government to engage us to reconsider a more measured approach and include our request for a comprehensive vetting process for the nominees.
"Our primary concern is to ensure that the office of the public prosecutor, which will have immense powers, including discretionary powers on discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA), maintains the highest standards of integrity, independence, accountability, and public confidence.
"The current proposed amendments have failed to address that concern," he said.
On March 3, the
Constitution (Amendment) (No.2) Bill 2026
, which seeks to separate the roles of the attorney-general and public prosecutor, was referred to the Parliamentary Special Select Committee.
The bill was first read on Feb 23.
If passed, it would formally separate the roles for the first time since independence, with the public prosecutor holding equal rank and status to the attorney-general.
Under the proposed framework, the public prosecutor would oversee all criminal prosecutions, while the attorney-general would serve as the government's legal adviser on matters excluding criminal law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azalina: Malaysia well placed to lead Islamic arbitration growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is well placed to help shape the growth of Islamic arbitration as Islamic finance, halal trade, wakaf development and cross-border investments expand.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the Asian International Arbitration Centre (AIAC) has played a key role in advancing arbitration, Syariah-compliant solutions and alternative dispute resolution regionally and internationally.
"Through its rules, institutional expertise, training initiatives and engagement with global stakeholders, the AIAC has strengthened Malaysia's reputation as a credible seat for arbitration," she said at the Islamic Arbitration Conference 2026 in Kuala Lumpur.
Also present was AIAC chief executive officer Datuk Almalena Sharmila Johan and Syariah Judiciary Department director-general and Syariah Chief Judge of Malaysia Mohd Amran Mat Zain.
Azalina said the growth of Islamic arbitration is a natural extension of that leadership, and requires greater cooperation among arbitral institutions, practitioners, scholars and policymakers across the Islamic world.
She said the future of Islamic arbitration depends on talent, legal certainty and innovation.
"Effective Islamic arbitration requires professionals who are conversant not only in arbitration law and procedures, but also in Shariah principles, Islamic commercial jurisprudence and international business," she said.
She said Malaysia has, over several decades, established itself as one of the world's leading centres for Islamic finance, while also playing a key role in driving the sector's growth in Asean.
"According to Bank Negara Malaysia's Annual Report 2025, Malaysia accounted for 82 per cent of Asean's Islamic banking assets, 91 per cent of takaful and retakaful assets, and 75 per cent of the region's outstanding sukuk value," she said.
Azalina said Malaysia's Islamic banking, takaful, sukuk and Syariah-compliant investment ecosystem is now recognised internationally.
She added that Malaysia was ranked the most developed Islamic finance market globally in the ICD-LSEG Islamic Finance Development Report 2024, ahead of Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
"This status corresponds to the progressive growth trajectory of the Islamic finance assets globally, expecting to mature from USD5.5 trillion in 2024 to US$7.5 trillion by 2028."
Azalina added that technology must be embraced, but not at the expense of fairness, due process, transparency and ethical responsibility.
"These values are central both to modern arbitration and to the Islamic legal tradition," she said.
She said Malaysia remains a neutral, arbitration-friendly jurisdiction, supported by a modern legislative framework, an independent judiciary and strong institutional support.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Water off a duck's back': Sweden's PM dodges slew of scandals
Article:
SWEDISH Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson has been dogged by scandal throughout his four-year term, wriggling out each time with little consequence, and now faces new corruption allegations three months ahead of legislative elections.
Headlines across Sweden in recent days have focused on questionable ethics from Kristersson and his wife, ordained Lutheran priest Birgitta Ed.
Newspaper Aftonbladet reported that a foundation run by Ed recruited volunteers to raise funds and refurbish the foundation's manor house in exchange for a "good network" of contacts and meetings held at the prime minister's official residence.
Two of the volunteers were later appointed to government positions, it said.
In a comment to Aftonbladet, Kristersson rejected the claims as "false accusations."
"This organisation is run by my wife and has nothing to do with me," he said.
Aftonbladet's reporter also said she was able to access Kristersson's and Ed's bedroom on the property without a security check.
Aftonbladet further revealed that the general director of Sweden's Public Health Agency was fired in 2024 after questioning a government directive to prioritise "existential health" – just as Ed started offering retreats for "existential health."
In another highly publicised case in 2023, Kristersson was heavily criticised for hiring a childhood friend to serve as national security advisor, and adamantly defended him when he left classified documents in an unlocked locker at a Stockholm conference centre.
Kristersson, whose right-wing government is propped up by the far-right, also faced heat in 2025 for backing his migration minister when the latter's 16-year-old son was found to have links to white supremacist groups.
He has also faced accusations of conflict of interest in his sister-in-law's business affairs, and was criticised for allowing his daughter to host a party at the prime minister's official summer residence.
Law professor Olle Lundin at Uppsala University said the slew of scandals were clear-cut cases of corruption.
"Corruption is when someone abuses their public power for their own or someone else's gain. And you almost have to say that he does that on a continuous basis," Lundin told AFP.
And yet there have been no legal consequences and very few calls for his resignation – in a country ranked the sixth-least corrupt in the world, according to Transparency International.
"There is nothing criminal, or illegal, in any of it," Lundin said. He said the only verdict would come from voters at the ballot box.
Opinion polls ahead of the Sept 13 general election show the right-wing bloc trailing the opposition by about 10 points.
"But they were there before the latest scandals," Nicklas Kallebring, head of opinion analysis at the Ipsos polling institute, told AFP.
The government has implemented a barrage of reforms to crack down on crime and immigration, including some hotly contested proposals – notably one to jail 13-year-olds accused of serious crimes – which have hurt the government in opinion polls, Kallebring said.
Social Democratic leader Magdalena Andersson, who is vying to take over as prime minister, said the scandals were evidence "of a corrupt Kristersson culture."
But University of Gothenburg political scientist Andreas Bagenholm said he didn't think they were big enough to hurt Kristersson politically.
"I don't know whether voters consider these as scandals, or rather as carelessness, poor judgment to some extent."
"There are no real juicy scandals where it's obvious that Kristersson has himself benefitted financially, so I don't think any of this will really stick to him," he said.
Corruption expert Lundin said Kristersson had not just been "careless", describing his actions as "very deliberate and intentional."
But even he acknowledged that scandals "run off him like water off a duck's back."
One of the few to call for Kristersson's resignation was Nalin Baksi, a Social Democratic political commentator who said Kristersson's friendships and frequent contacts with top businessmen and lobbyists ought to "immediately disqualify" him for the job of prime minister.
"Time and again, reports have emerged about conflicts of interest, financial benefits for his family, and suspected abuse of power," she wrote in the Expressen newspaper last Thursday.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rubio faces tough task of selling Iran reset to wary Gulf allies
Article:
ABU DHABI: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio faces a delicate mission this week pitching Washington's Iran peace deal to Gulf Arab leaders who fear excessive concessions will strengthen Teheran and reshape the region's security balance and oil flows.
Rubio will meet them in the United Arab Emirates on Tuesday, before traveling to Kuwait and Bahrain, where he will meet officials from the Gulf Cooperation Council, a grouping of monarchies that also includes Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Oman.
At issue are elements of a draft agreement that include no limits on Iran's ballistic missiles, a proposed US$300 billion reconstruction fund and provisions that could expand Tehran's regional influence and control over critical oil shipping lanes.
All six GCC nations are strategic US allies that offered some degree of logistical support to Washington during the US-Israeli war with Iran that began four months ago and all were buffeted by Iranian airstrikes as a result.
Some of those countries are feeling privately disappointed - and surprised - by an interim deal that could open the door to US normalisation with Iran, a predominantly Shia country that most Sunni GCC states consider their main adversary.
The opinions of these nations matter to US policymakers.
The UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain all host US military bases that in turn make up the backbone of America's security architecture in the Middle East.
Should any of those countries rethink their security relationship with the US, even in a subtle way, it could have a significant impact on US military strategy in the region.
For Rubio personally, the trip requires a balancing act.
While America's top diplomat needs to assuage regional allies, he must do so without appearing to criticise the US-Iran memorandum of understanding.
President Donald Trump, who signed the accord last week, remains firmly behind it despite criticism from some of his fellow Republicans in Congress who have accused the administration of capitulating to Teheran.
Andrew Peek, a former deputy assistant secretary of state for Iraq and Iran who served on Trump's National Security Council during both his terms, argued that Rubio could reassure any nervous allies by pointing out that Trump has a history of being tough on the Islamic Republic.
"I think you can just remind them that the president has conducted extremely hawkish policies toward Iran - and if this MoU falls through, he will have no compunction about going back to striking them," said Peek, who is now at the Atlantic Council think tank.
Leaders from all GCC countries hosting Rubio or present at this week's talks at least publicly pushed for a diplomatic solution before the war kicked off in February.
Most also pushed for a diplomatic off-ramp during the conflict, even as they in practice facilitated the US war effort.
Still, the specific terms of the MoU privately shocked regional officials, according to analysts and diplomats.
One concern relates to ballistic missiles. Throughout the war, the Trump administration said that destroying Iran's ballistic missile capacity was a central goal.
That objective aligned with the interests of the Sunni Gulf states as - unlike the US - all are well within Iran's ballistic range and have been targeted by Iranian missiles.
The MoU, however, does not mention Iranian missiles at all, and Trump himself has in recent days said that denying Teheran such weapons would be "unfair."
The MoU also foresees a US$300 billion reconstruction fund for Teheran, which regional neighbours fear could allow the Islamic Republic to build up its military capacity, while increasing support for regional proxy groups that could destabilise governments throughout the region.
Bahrain's mainly Sunni leadership, in particular, is concerned that a well-funded Iran could foment an uprising among the island nation's mainly Shia populace, analysts say.
During the Arab Spring, the nation of roughly 1.65 million was the site of massive recurring street protests.
Iran has denied any covert attempts to stoke unrest but has publicly expressed support for Bahraini Shia activists in the past.
The accord, as written, also appears to concede that Iran could have a key role in controlling the Strait of Hormuz going forward, a major concern for Kuwait, Qatar and Saudi Arabia, which rely on the strait to export oil and gas.
More broadly, US officials have begun speaking about a broader reset for Teheran, a potential transformation that most GCC states are wary of. On Saturday, Vice President JD Vance said the US was willing to "fundamentally transform" its relationship with Teheran.
"The agreement rehabilitates Teheran's regime as a regional power," veteran Saudi columnist Abdulrahman Al-Rashed wrote in Saudi English-language daily Arab News last week.
"Most of the funds Teheran will acquire in the coming weeks are likely to go primarily towards strengthening the military position, not to support living conditions or the Iranian economy."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari Ghani: Malaysia risked higher tariffs if US deal wasn't signed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia would have risked facing higher tariffs if a deal was not signed with the United States last year when other countries were hit with rates as high as 145 per cent.
Investment, Trade and Industry Minister, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani told Parliament today that Malaysia risks facing adverse impacts on its trade competitiveness if it does not continue engaging with the United States (US) over reciprocal tariffs and other trade measures.
"When there is no engagement, we will face the risk of the US considering these tariffs as unilateral.
"By unilateral, it means when we export goods, we already state the price to US importers. When the goods arrive in the US, they will impose an additional 19 per cent, which is passed on to their consumers, not us.
"But the impact on us is that our products, although competitive, become uncompetitive due to the additional 19 per cent imposed by the US.
"We cannot accept this, but that is their right," he said when replying to a supplementary question from Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin (PN–Larut) during the oral question-and-answer session in the Dewan Rakyat.
He said the government acknowledged that trade with the US is crucial in strengthening the country's economy, while ensuring that the electrical and electronics (E&E) and semiconductor sectors continue to grow.
"A large portion of our exports goes to the US. Out of RM1.6 trillion in exports, RM711 billion comes from the E&E and semiconductor sectors.
"To the US alone, we export RM233 billion annually. We also ensure these exports do not negatively affect our economy.
"That is why we need to engage with them. Initially, I disagreed as well. But when I looked at the situation—for example, China being hit with tariffs of 145 per cent—that is very high.
"If Malaysia were hit with even 40 per cent at that time, what would happen to our companies trading with them?" he said.
Johari also said the 10 per cent tariff under Section 122 of the US Trade Act 1974 is only valid for 150 days and is scheduled to end on 24 July.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No room for excuses: Salleh says every Pan Borneo delay drains Sabah’s coffers and patience
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — Inspector General of Projects Sabah Datuk Seri Dr Salleh Said Keruak reaffirmed his commitment to closely monitoring the implementation of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project, stressing that every delay carries a cost to both government expenditure and public benefit.
Speaking after a site visit on Tuesday with officials from the Public Works Department (JKR), contractors and relevant agencies, Salleh said several key work packages (WP) under the project were making encouraging progress and remained on track to achieve major milestones this year.
“I am encouraged by the progress achieved so far. WP 3, 4, 10 and 29 are on track to obtain their Certificates of Partial Completion (CPC) this year,” he said.
A major milestone was recorded under WP4 with the opening of the new four-lane dual carriageway between the Bongawan flyover and Benoni flyover on June 15. The new section is expected to significantly improve traffic flow and enhance road safety for motorists travelling along the route.
Salleh also said WP6 and WP7 are expected to complete their original scope of works, achieve CPC status and open the route from the Putatan flyover to the Sepanggar interchange by October this year.
“The remaining five Phase 1A packages are scheduled for completion between 2027 and 2028. While progress is encouraging, there should be no room for avoidable delays,” he said.
He emphasised that project delays not only increase construction costs but also postpone the benefits that infrastructure projects are intended to deliver to the people.
“Every delay comes with a cost. It increases project expenses and delays the economic and social benefits that the people of Sabah deserve,” he said.
